api/spec/packages/aip/common/definitions.purpose: Shared OpenAPI YAML component library for the v3 AIP spec: reusable filter schemas, RFC 7807 error responses, cursor pagination metadata, security schemes, and primitive property types referenced via $ref by all AIP routes. These are raw OpenAPI YAML fragments consumed by the TypeSpec build pipeline, not TypeSpec files.

patterns.name: RFC 7807 error hierarchy via allOf BaseError
patterns.description: Every error schema extends BaseError (status, title, instance, detail required) via allOf; BadRequestError adds invalid_parameters, others add example values only. Never add a top-level error schema without extending BaseError.

patterns.name: Filter composition via oneOf over named atomic sub-types
patterns.description: Composite filters (StringFieldFilter, NumericFieldFilter, DateTimeFieldFilter) are oneOf over named sub-types each with additionalProperties:false. New variants must be a named sub-type first, then listed in the parent oneOf — never inline anonymous objects.

patterns.name: x-examples required on every filter schema
patterns.description: Every filter schema carries x-examples covering all variant forms; missing them produce incomplete SDK examples.

patterns.name: Cursor pagination via CursorMeta family
patterns.description: Paginated responses reference CursorMeta, CursorMetaWithTotal, or CursorMetaWithEstimatedTotal from metadatas.yaml; never inline page metadata. CursorMetaPage requires size, next, previous (nullable strings).

patterns.name: readOnly:true on server-generated fields; UUID_RW for writable IDs
patterns.description: UUID, CreatedAt, UpdatedAt and all error fields are readOnly:true; UUID_RW is the writable ID variant. New response-only fields must carry readOnly:true.
patterns.example: UUID: {format: uuid, readOnly: true}  vs  UUID_RW: {format: uuid}  # no readOnly

patterns.name: x-flatten-allOf:true on specialised ID types
patterns.description: ID types composed via allOf (UserId, TeamId, OrganizationId, NullableTimestamp) include x-flatten-allOf:true to collapse the allOf wrapper in SDK output.

patterns.name: Security schemes only in security.yaml
patterns.description: All bearer/cookie security schemes live in security.yaml; internal ones carry x-internal:true. Never inline a securityScheme in a route file.
patterns.example: clientToken: {x-internal: true, type: http, scheme: bearer, bearerFormat: Token}

key_file_guides.role: All reusable query-filter schemas: String/Numeric/DateTime/Uuid/Boolean FieldFilter, SortQuery, Labels/PublicLabels/AttributesFieldFilter, and atomic sub-types.
key_file_guides.watch_for: UuidFieldFilter reuses StringFieldEquals/OEQ/NEQ sub-types — do not duplicate. Adding a variant without x-examples gives incomplete SDK docs.

key_file_guides.role: Full error tree: ErrorResponse status-discriminated union, per-status responses, BaseError, and InvalidParameter variants (Standard/Min/Max/Choice/Dependent).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding an HTTP error code requires updating both the oneOf list and discriminator.mapping. KonnectCPLegacy* is a parallel plain-JSON family — do not cross-reference with BaseError descendants.

key_file_guides.role: Cursor pagination schemas (CursorMetaPage, CursorMeta, CursorMetaWithTotal/EstimatedTotal/SizeAndTotal) and page query params (CursorPageQuery, PageBefore/After/Size/Number).
key_file_guides.watch_for: CursorMetaWithSizeAndTotal differs from CursorMetaWithTotal (total may be null on non-first pages). Do not remove x-speakeasy-terraform-ignore on PageNumber/PageSize/PaginatedMeta.

key_file_guides.role: Primitive property schemas (UUID, UUID_RW, CreatedAt, UpdatedAt, ExpiresAt, NullableTimestamp) and allOf+x-flatten-allOf ID types (UserId, PrincipalId, TeamId, OrganizationId).
key_file_guides.watch_for: UUID is readOnly; use UUID_RW for user-settable IDs. ID types need x-flatten-allOf:true.

key_file_guides.role: Konnect-specific schemas: NullableUUID, Labels/LabelsUpdate, PublicLabels/PublicLabelsUpdate, EntityType, path params (AuditLogDestinationId, Workspace).
key_file_guides.watch_for: LabelsUpdate/PublicLabelsUpdate are writeOnly nullable variants — do not collapse read and write forms into one schema.

key_file_guides.role: Central registry of all securitySchemes (personalAccessToken, systemAccountAccessToken, konnectAccessToken, portalAccessToken, serviceAccessToken, clientToken).
key_file_guides.watch_for: clientToken carries x-internal:true — keep it to prevent public SDK exposure. Defining a scheme here is not enough; it must be referenced by routes.

anti_patterns: Inline anonymous filter objects inside a oneOf instead of a named sub-type with additionalProperties:false
Error schemas not extending BaseError via allOf — breaks RFC 7807 and the ErrorResponse discriminator
Omitting x-examples on new filter schemas
Adding security schemes in route files instead of security.yaml
Mixing KonnectCPLegacy* schemas with standard BaseError descendants

decisions.decision: Filter types use oneOf over named atomic sub-types
decisions.rationale: Named sub-types let SDK generators produce distinct type names (e.g. NumericFieldLTFilter) and enable $ref reuse without duplication.

decisions.decision: Pagination split into CursorMeta vs WithTotal vs WithEstimatedTotal
decisions.rationale: Endpoints have different cost profiles for exact vs estimated counts; one schema would over-compute or mislead clients about accuracy.

decisions.decision: KonnectCPLegacy* schemas coexist with RFC 7807 BaseError
decisions.rationale: Legacy Kong gateway endpoints return plain {message} JSON while AIP endpoints return application/problem+json; both must be expressible without polluting each other.

api/spec/packages/aip/lib/rules.purpose: Custom TypeSpec linter rules enforcing AIP naming conventions and structural constraints for the v3 API spec. Rules run during make gen-api (tsp compile) and block (error) or warn before any OpenAPI/SDK artifact is generated.

patterns.name: createRule factory for every rule
patterns.description: Every rule is exported via createRule from @typespec/compiler returning a visitor-callback object; never raw functions or classes.
patterns.example: export const myRule = createRule({ name: 'my-rule', severity: 'warning', messages: { default: paramMessage`...` }, create: (context) => ({ model: (node) => { ... } }) })

patterns.name: Two-tier severity: error blocks, warning informs
patterns.description: Rules whose violations corrupt wire format or SDK method names (enum values snake_case, @friendlyName presence) use severity:'error'; style/doc rules use 'warning'.

patterns.name: Casing contracts by node type
patterns.description: Model names PascalCase; non-path properties snake_case; $path params camelCase; enum names/members PascalCase; enum member values snake_case; @operationId kebab-case. The $path decorator is checked before choosing camelCase vs snake_case.

patterns.name: @friendlyName required, inverted for *Endpoints/*Operations
patterns.description: All named models/enums/unions/interfaces require @friendlyName EXCEPT interfaces ending in Endpoints or Operations, which must NOT have it. Violation severity is 'error'.

patterns.name: Casing helpers live only in utils.js
patterns.description: isPascalCaseNoAcronyms/isCamelCaseNoAcronyms/isSnakeCase/isKebabCase are exported from utils.js with a PascalCase exceptions list (OAuth2, URL, API, UI, ID). Rule files import them; never inline regexes.

patterns.name: field-prefix EXCLUDED_PREFIXES governs exemptions
patterns.description: When 2+ fields share an underscore prefix a grouping warning fires; AIP-standard boolean prefixes (is, enable, disable, allow, etc.) are exempt via the EXCLUDED_PREFIXES array.
patterns.example: EXCLUDED_PREFIXES in field-prefix.js — add new globally-exempt prefixes here

patterns.name: Composition over inheritance
patterns.description: extends without @discriminator on the base triggers a warning; composition-over-inheritance.js uses isTemplateInstance() to give a different message for template-based vs plain extension.
patterns.example: model Foo { ...BaseFields; } // OK   vs   model Foo extends Base {} // warning unless @discriminator on Base

key_file_guides.role: Single source for casing helpers and doc-comment string helpers (extractMarkdownFromDocComment, wrapMarkdownAsDocComment, detectNewline, getIndentBefore); holds the PascalCase exceptions list.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Add new acronyms (e.g. that the regex wrongly flags) to the exceptions list here; never inline regexes in rule files.

key_file_guides.role: Two rules: casing (warning) for name casing; casing-aip-errors (error) for enum member values being snake_case. The modelProperty visitor checks $path before camelCase vs snake_case.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Forgetting the $path check produces false positives on path params.

key_file_guides.role: Error-severity rule requiring @friendlyName on named types; INVERTED for *Endpoints/*Operations interfaces.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Getting the inversion wrong (flagging Endpoints interfaces for missing @friendlyName) is the most common mistake.

key_file_guides.role: doc-decorator (warning on missing @doc) and doc-format (async Prettier formatting of doc bodies). Models ending in Response are exempt from property @doc; properties named _ or contentType always exempt.
key_file_guides.watch_for: doc-format is async (Prettier 3.x), declares async:true and batch-processes DocNodes in an exit() hook — do not make it sync.

key_file_guides.role: Warns when 2+ fields share an underscore prefix; EXCLUDED_PREFIXES exempts AIP boolean-flag prefixes.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Globally-exempt prefixes must go in EXCLUDED_PREFIXES or they warn on all uses.

key_file_guides.role: Warns against `field: T | null`; prefer `field?: T`. Only fires on non-optional union properties including null.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Checks !property.node?.optional — optional `| null` properties are intentionally not flagged.

key_file_guides.role: Warns on extends without @discriminator; isTemplateInstance() distinguishes template vs plain bases (messageId 'instance' vs 'default').
key_file_guides.watch_for: Keep both message variants in sync.

anti_patterns: Defining rules as raw functions instead of createRule
Using `| null` on properties instead of the optional `?` modifier
Adding a field-prefix exemption inline instead of in EXCLUDED_PREFIXES
Using extends for composition without @discriminator on the base

decisions.decision: Error severity for wire-format/SDK-name constraints; warning for stylistic
decisions.rationale: Enum values must be snake_case (JSON wire) and @operationId kebab-case (SDK method names) — violations corrupt artifacts; doc/composition rules are warnings to allow incremental adoption.

decisions.decision: PascalCase exceptions list (OAuth2, URL, API, UI, ID) in utils.js
decisions.rationale: AIP acknowledges common industry acronyms; without the list, names like APIKey would be wrongly flagged.

decisions.decision: field-prefix exempts AIP boolean-flag prefixes via EXCLUDED_PREFIXES
decisions.rationale: AIP defines standard boolean prefixes (is_active, enable_x) that intentionally repeat; grouping them would violate AIP guidance.

api/spec/packages/aip/scripts.purpose: Post-processing Node.js ESM scripts that fix TypeSpec-generated OpenAPI YAML before SDK generators and runtime validators consume it. Each is a standalone CLI tool invoked by make gen-api after tsp compile.

patterns.name: YAML round-trip with fixed formatting
patterns.description: Each script reads with YAML.parse, mutates in place, then writes with YAML.stringify(doc, { indent: 2, lineWidth: 0 }). lineWidth:0 is mandatory to avoid spurious line-wrap diffs.
patterns.example: const parsed = await readYamlFile(filePath); if (flattenAllOf(parsed)) await writeYamlFile(filePath, parsed)

patterns.name: Idempotency marker x-flatten-allOf
patterns.description: flatten-allof.mjs stamps x-flatten-allOf:true on processed nodes to prevent double-processing on re-runs; the x- prefix is ignored by OpenAPI tooling. Check the marker before mutating.
patterns.example: if (node[FLATTEN_MARKER] !== true) { node[FLATTEN_MARKER] = true; return true; } return false

patterns.name: Recursive walk returning changed boolean
patterns.description: Transform functions recurse the YAML tree and return a boolean; main() skips the write when changed === false to avoid no-op file touches.
patterns.example: for (const value of Object.values(node)) changed = flattenAllOf(value) || changed

patterns.name: additionalProperties:{not:{}} -> false rewrite
patterns.description: seal-object-schemas.mjs rewrites the TypeSpec seal-object-schemas form `{ not: {} }` to `false` because kin-openapi's deepObject query decoder cannot handle the not:{} form.

patterns.name: apply-doc-fixes uses the TypeSpec compiler API
patterns.description: apply-doc-fixes.mjs calls compile() and applyCodeFixes() directly (not the CLI), deriving the entry point from package.json#exports['.'].typespec and ruleset from package.json#name so renaming the package stays correct.
patterns.example: const program = await compile(NodeHost, entry, { noEmit: true, linterRuleSet: { extends: [RULESET] } }); await applyCodeFixes(NodeHost, fixes)

key_file_guides.role: Moves sibling properties on allOf-with-$ref schemas into a new allOf branch for well-formed SDK output. Invoked as `node flatten-allof.mjs api/v3/openapi.yaml`.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Only processes nodes whose allOf contains a $ref; x-* keys are never moved; removing the marker check causes infinite loops on re-run.

key_file_guides.role: Rewrites additionalProperties:{not:{}} to false across one or more YAML files (supports globs, dedups via a Set).
key_file_guides.watch_for: isNotEmptyObject is precise: exactly one key `not` whose value is an empty plain object.

key_file_guides.role: Applies the TypeSpec format-doc-comment codefix in place via the compiler API.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Exits 0 (not 1) when there are no fixes — callers must not treat exit 0 as failure. Only applies fixes whose id === 'format-doc-comment', skipping the auto-attached suppress codefix.

anti_patterns: Adding business logic or spec-authoring code here — this is strictly a post-processing layer
Changing YAML_OPTIONS.lineWidth from 0 — produces spurious line-wrap diffs
Removing the x-flatten-allOf idempotency marker check — causes infinite loops on re-run
Introducing a build/transpilation step — scripts run directly as ESM .mjs
Mutating TypeSpec source files in api/spec/packages/ — these scripts only post-process generated YAML

decisions.decision: Scripts are standalone ESM .mjs with no build step
decisions.rationale: Keeps the toolchain simple — make gen-api invokes them as plain `node script.mjs`, no bundler or transpilation.

decisions.decision: The idempotency marker uses the x- vendor extension prefix
decisions.rationale: OpenAPI tooling ignores x- keys, so the marker survives downstream validation without unknown-field errors.

decisions.decision: additionalProperties:{not:{}} is rewritten to false, not removed
decisions.rationale: Preserves the closed-schema semantics TypeSpec intended; `false` is semantically equivalent and accepted by kin-openapi's deepObject decoder.
